title: 10 best herbal teas for upset stomach

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile teas helps with upset stomach because of its soothing and calming properties. The flavonoids present in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have anti-inflammatory effects that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. Additionally, chamomile's apigenic action binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms in the stomach, which can alleviate symptoms of upset stomach such as cramps, bloating, and diarrhea. This natural remedy promotes a sense of calm and relaxation, making it an effective solution for calming an upset stomach.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin, can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms like bloating, cramps, and diarrhea.

Antispasmodic properties: The essential oils present in chamomile tea, including bisabolol, have a calming effect on the muscles of the stomach and intestines, which can help relieve spasms, cramps, and discomfort associated with upset stomach.

Digestive soothing properties: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenes can help soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms like nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation in the gut.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to soothe and calm digestive issues, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain a number of bioactive compounds that help to relax the muscles in the stomach, alleviating symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for every eight ounces of water.
Heat fresh filtered water in a tea kettle or pot to boiling point for approximately five minutes.
Remove from heat and let steep the chamomilla flowers in the hot water for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the herbal solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming and enjoy within thirty minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with upset stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The warm liquid relaxes the stomach muscles, reducing cramps and spasms that can cause discomfort. Additionally, ginger's carminative properties help eliminate trapped gas, providing quick relief from bloating and indigestion. As a result, herbal ginger teas have been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of nausea, heartburn, and other stomach upset issues, making it a natural and effective remedy for maintaining digestive harmony.
remedy_properties: Digestive enzymes: Ginger tea contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have been shown to stimulate digestive enzyme production, helping to break down food and alleviate symptoms of indigestion and nausea.

Antioxidants: Ginger tea is rich in antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by inflammation and oxidative stress, reducing the risk of ulcers and other stomach problems.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and curcumin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate symptoms of conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stem of the ginger plant, rhyzomes contain compounds that help alleviate nausea and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
Roots: Ginger roots are rich in fiber, which can help regulate bowel movements and soothe digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Chop one-inch piece of fresh ginger root into small pieces.
Boil one quart of water and add two teaspoons of chopped ginger.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until ginger is tender.
Strain tea leaves and discard solids, adding honey to taste as desired.
Serve warm and enjoy within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel teas helps with upset stomach because its natural oils, such as anethole, have a calming effect on the digestive system. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce bloating and cramping, while its carminative properties aid in the release of gas from the body. Additionally, fennel tea can help to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and diarrhea by increasing the production of stomach enzymes and improving gut motility.
remedy_properties: Carminative properties: Fennel tea helps to expel gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort. This property also helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, easing spasms and cramps.
Antispasmodic properties: Fennel tea's antispasmodic compounds help to relieve smooth muscle spasms in the digestive tract, providing relief from symptoms like abdominal pain, cramps, and diarrhea.

Digestive enzyme stimulant properties: Fennel tea contains compounds that stimulate the production of digestive enzymes in the body, helping to improve digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can alleviate symptoms of upset stomach such as indigestion and nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the fennel plant have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues and calm inflammation in the stomach, making them a popular choice for medicinal teas.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain compounds that help reduce gas and bloating, making them a natural remedy for relieving upset stomachs and promoting digestion.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried fennel seeds or one-fourth cup of fresh fennel leaves into a heat-resistant teapot.
Boil one cup of water in the kettle and pour it over the fennel seeds or leaves in the teapot.
Steep the fennel mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and oils to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature, which is typically around 15-20 minutes after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with upset stomach because of its natural calming properties. The menthol content in peppermint relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause discomfort. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties soothe the digestive lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to an upset stomach. As a result, drinking herbal peppermint tea may help alleviate symptoms such as bloating, gas, and nausea, promoting a sense of relief and comfort for those experiencing digestive issues.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Peppermint tea helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can exacerbate symptoms of indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
Carminative properties: Peppermint tea's carminative properties help to prevent the formation of gas bubbles in the stomach and intestines, reducing bloating and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to conditions like gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) and stomach ulcers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in menthol and other volatile oils that help to soothe the digestive tract and relieve symptoms of indigestion.

remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried peppermint leaves and measure out one teaspoon for every eight ounces of water.
Boil filtered water in a teapot or kettle and let it cool down to a simmer for five minutes.
Add the measured peppermint leaves to the pot and steep for ten to fifteen minutes to release the oils.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids immediately.
Allow the tea to cool down slightly before drinking, sipping slowly to help soothe the stomach discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried carum carvi leaves and seeds in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Measure two teaspoons of dried carum carvi mixture or four teaspoons of fresh leaves into a tea infuser.
Combine the herb mixture with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or heat-resistant cup.
Steep the herbal mixture for five to seven minutes, then strain the liquid using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Consume the warm tea immediately after preparation, ideally three times a day, to alleviate upset stomach symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried english lavender flowers for every eight ounces of boiling water.

Alpha acids: The alpha acids in hops, such as humulone, have a sedative effect on the digestive system, helping to calm cramps, spasms, and diarrhea associated with upset stomach, while also promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the hops plant have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation, making them a great addition to teas for upset stomach.
Buds: Hops buds are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help calm irritations in the stomach and alleviate symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh humulus lupulus leaves and flowers or purchase dried ones.
Measure one teaspoon of dried hops or two teaspoons of fresh hops per cup of boiling water.
Steep the hops in boiling water for five to seven minutes depending on desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before consuming and enjoy within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with upset stomach because it contains anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds that calm the digestive tract. The natural anthraquinones in plantain tea stimulate the movement of food through the digestive system, reducing symptoms of indigestion and bloating. Additionally, the astringent properties of plantain help to absorb excess moisture and reduce inflammation, providing relief from cramps and spasms in the stomach. Overall, herbal plantain teas offer a gentle and effective way to soothe an upset stomach and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain tea contains flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as bloating and cramps.

Antioxidant properties: Plantain tea is rich in antioxidants, which neutralize free radicals that can damage the stomach lining and contribute to conditions like gastritis and ulcers, promoting a healthy gut environment.

Softpennings and mucilages: Plantain contains softpennings and mucilages, which are natural demulcent properties that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach, providing relief from irritation, discomfort, and heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots are known to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation in the gut.
Barks: The bark of the plantain plant contains tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and tighten the digestive tract, making it a popular choice for teas addressing upset stomachs.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried plantago major leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Heat one cup of boiling water in a teapot or infuser for five minutes.
Add the gathered plantago major leaves to the boiling water and steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool for two to three minutes before drinking and consuming within thirty minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for upset stomach are a natural and soothing way to calm digestive issues, providing relief from cramps, bloating, and discomfort. These teas work by gently easing inflammation in the gut and promoting digestion, reducing symptoms of indigestion and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Examples of herbal teas that help with upset stomach include peppermint, chamomile, ginger, and fennel. Drinking these teas can improve daily life by providing quick relief from digestive discomfort, allowing individuals to focus on their day rather than managing symptoms.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat upset stomach with herbal teas is to choose calming and soothing options like peppermint, chamomile, or licorice root tea. These herbs help alleviate digestive issues by reducing inflammation, calming the gut, and promoting relaxation. Drink 1-2 cups of tea after meals or before bedtime to relieve discomfort and promote a restful night's sleep. Regular consumption can also help prevent future stomach upset.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to upset stomach that are treated with herbal teas are digestive issues such as bloating, indigestion, and diarrhea. Herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger have natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the gut lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Other herbs like licorice root, fennel, and dandelion root also help to ease symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), gastritis, and stomach ulcers, promoting overall digestive health and well-being.
